varieties.name: Pratia pedunculata (White Star Creeper)
varieties.origin: Native to Australia (SE Queensland to NSW)
varieties.traits: Low-growing mat-forming perennial, 2-10cm height, spreads to 1.5m width. Tiny rounded green leaves form dense carpet.
varieties.availability: Widely available at Queensland nurseries including Garden Express, Australian Plants Online, SE QLD Plants, Plant Tiles Australia, Sunshine Coast Nurseries.

varieties.name: Viola hederacea / Viola banksii (Native Violet) 🌏
varieties.origin: Native to eastern Australia (SE Queensland to Tasmania). V. banksii discovered 1770 at Botany Bay by Joseph Banks and Daniel Solander.
varieties.traits: Low evergreen groundcover 5-15cm height, spreads to 2m via underground runners. Kidney-shaped to heart-shaped bright green leaves.
varieties.yield_notes: Profuse flowering creating "sea of flowers" effect. Edible flowers for salads and garnish.
varieties.availability: Widely available throughout Queensland including Australian Plants Online, Birkett Gardens Brisbane, The Plant Hub, Sunshine Coast Plants, Daley's Fruit Tree Nursery.

varieties.name: Jasminum sambac 'Maid of Orleans' (Mogra, Motiya, Bela) 🌏
varieties.origin: Native to India and Southeast Asia. Traditional cultivar, cultivated for centuries. Most common form of Arabian jasmine.
varieties.traits: Vigorous vining shrub or climber, 1-3m height. Can be pruned to dense shrub form. Dark green glossy opposite leaves. Fast-growing, evergreen.
